branch_cards.likely_gain: capture the most bounded reusable law while the GitHub-only endcap and reality-sync evidence are still sharp
stop_conditions: stop if the next reviewed remote-owner landing differs materially enough that the law is still unstable
stop if the candidate starts widening into scenario canon instead of bounded practice law

branch_cards.likely_gain: preserve the full wave-gated remediation closure route before future repetitions blur planning and merge discipline
branch_cards.likely_cost: broader authoring and upkeep overhead than the technique candidate
stop_conditions: stop if local and GitHub-only closure tails are still diverging too much for one scenario seed
stop if the route starts depending on repo-specific exceptions rather than stable stage boundaries

branch_cards.likely_gain: force an explicit checkpoint-aware judgment about whether the repeated delivery lane should stay manual or become a future automation seed
branch_cards.likely_cost: classification pressure before a second full reviewed closure cycle exists
stop_conditions: stop if repeat evidence still comes only from this one closure cycle
stop if approval-sensitive mutation surfaces remain too variable for a stable automation seed shape

hold_option.reason: one full closure cycle is enough for donor harvest, but a second reviewed pass would make broader promotion and automation claims safer
